baseline.reason: Correctly identifies UAF but wrongly claims eval_tuple is called twice on the same rettv (lines 283 and 292 are mutually exclusive if/else branches). Misidentifies the trigger path — the real issue is the shallow copy into the tuple followed by tuple_free leaving rettv dangling, not a 'second call to eval_tuple'. Also incorrectly suggests rettv->vval.v_tuple was set before being freed; rettv_tuple_set is never called on the error path.

vastdb.reason: Accurately identifies the shallow copy mechanism in tuple_set_item (bad.c:1438), the rettv->tuple sharing of heap data, tuple_free freeing that shared data, and rettv retaining a dangling pointer. Trigger path line references are correct. Minor omission: doesn't discuss that the fix is an early-return guard on eval1 failure, but the root-cause and trigger-path description is fundamentally sound and matches the actual CVE-2025-55157 mechanism.
